title: VP of Software Engineering
description:
VP of Software Engineering
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

As VP of Software Engineering, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining a high-performing, scalable engineering organization across Product Development, Ops, and Technical Support. This is a role for someone who’s hands-on enough to spot an issue i

Electrical Designers 

We’re growing our Design & Consulting team and looking for talented Electrical Designers to help shape the future of shipbuilding! 

 

As a Designer at Genoa, you’ll be at the forefront of major marine projects, transforming complex concepts into precise production designs and immersive 3D models. From powering innovative vessels to solving real-world engineering challenges, your work will directly support the creation of world-class ships.

 

Whether you’re launching your career or bringing years of expertise, you’ll collaborate with a passionate team, work with cutting-edge tools, and play a hands-on role in bringing ambitious designs to life with accuracy, creativity, and impact.

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modeling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence.

 

Proudly supporting major initiatives like Canada’s National Shipbuilding Strategy (NSS) and commercial projects across North America, we foster an environment where collaboration drives innovation, learning and development are continuous, and every team member plays a vital role in shaping our future.

Piping Designers

 

We're seeking skilled and detail-oriented Piping Designers, of all levels, to join our Design & Consulting team in supporting innovative shipbuilding and marine engineering projects!

 

Our team is split into two main divisions that complement each other, 3D modeling and Manufacturing information (MI). The 3D model team takes engineering documents and turns them into a fully integrated digital ship. The MI team takes the 3D model and produces 2D drawings that the shipyard production facilities can fabricate from.

 

In this role, you’ll play a key part in developing piping systems and layouts for new vessel construction and refit programs. Working closely with other disciplines, production teams, and regulatory bodies, you’ll help bring complex designs to life using advanced 3D modeling tools and industry software to produce 2D drawings.

 

ABOUT US

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modeling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence.

Northwood has been recognized for leading change in continuing care, advancing innovative models, embracing technology, and prioritizing dignity, choice, and belonging. title: Medicare Advisor
description:
Medicare Advisor
Location

USA; Brooklyn, NYC

Employment Type

Full time

Location Type

Remote

Department

New Ventures

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

Propel serves approximately 1M users who are Medicare eligible, most of whom are also Medicaid eligible. We are building the first-ever app-based brokerage focused on helping "duals" (Americans on Medicare and Medicaid) enroll in healthcare plans. We specialize in Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) and are expanding to 30 states in 2026, with plans to move into larger Medicare markets over the next 24 months. As a Medicare Advisor, you will be a trusted expert, helping users compare Medicare plans, enroll

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

About the Role

We're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.

This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.

description:
Home
Careers

Founding Sales Lead
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

Help build the commercial engine of an AI-powered safety company with real product, real customers, and industrial pilots deploying this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. We've shipped product to 16 countries, we're well-funded, and we're deploying to industrial pilot sites this summer across oil & gas, marine, manufacturing, and heavy industry.

We're looking for a Founding Sales Lead to help us build our sales motion from the ground up. This isn't cold-calling from a script, it's figuring out how to sell a category-defining product into industries where the buyers are safety managers, operations leads, and EHS directors.


‍

What You’ll Do

This is not a traditional BDR role, it’s a chance to help build the commercial engine of a deep-tech startup.

You will:

Lead sales outreach, qualification, and discovery with enterprise and mid-market industrial customers.
Run customer discovery to deeply understand problems, workflows, and buying processes.
Help shape our storytelling, messaging, and positioning based on real customer insights.
Work with the founding team to build and refine landing pages, funnels, and experiments to test what resonates.
Drive top-of-funnel pipeline, schedule demos, and support enterprise sales cycles.
Collaborate with marketing to grow ou

title: Founding Firmware Developer
description:
Home
Careers
Contact
View All Openings
Founding Firmware Developer
St. John’s, NL (Hybrid)

First firmware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated firmware developer, you won't just write code, you'll own the firmware architecture, make critical decisions about reliability, connectivity, and power management, and ship software that performs in industrial conditions where failure isn't an option. You'll work directly with our CEO and CTO.



‍AI-Native Development

We're an AI-native team. You'll use Claude Code, Cursor, and Copilot daily to accelerate development, generating drivers, test harnesses, utilities, and boilerplate, then critically review and harden that code for production embedded systems. AI isn't a replacement for deep embedded engineering judgment. It's a multiplier.

First hardware hire at an AI-powered safety company with a major oil & gas contract and industrial pilots launching this summer.

Audyse is building intelligent PPE that restores clear communication and real-time situational awareness in the world's loudest, most dangerous workplaces. Our headsets suppress harmful noise while preserving speech, alarms, and environmental cues, paired with a software platform that gives safety teams live exposure data and actionable insights.

We've already shipped 400 consumer headsets to 16 countries, completed 4 hardware revisions in 12 months, and navigated international manufacturing from Newfoundland. We've secured a major R&D contract and have 5 industrial pilots committed for this summer.

As our first dedicated hardware developer, you'll own the hardware stack. You'll design boards, bring them up in the lab, validate them in the field, and ensure they perform reliably on offshore rigs, factory floors, and vessel decks.
‍

Design and iterate on schematics and PCB layouts for compact, industrial-grade IoT headset.
Conduct lab bring-up, debug boards, and validate hardware functionality.
Build test setups and collect data to inform hardware and firmware design decisions.
Collaborate with firmware, software, and product teams to ensure seamless integration.

About Granville Biomedical
Granville Biomedical is a Canadian health tech company dedicated to advancing women’s pelvic and reproductive health through the design and commercialization of innovative anatomical models and educational tools. Our mission is to empower clinicians, educators, and patients globally with products that promote health equity, evidence-based care, and inclusive design.

Over the past year, we have made significant progress by expanding internationally, launching new proprietary products, and building strategic partnerships in women’s health. We are now seeking a highly motivated and self-driven Women's Health Sales & Business Development Lead to help amplify our global impact and take our growth to the next level.

This is a full-time 6-month contract position with an opportunity for extension. Based on funding requirements, preference will be given to candidates based in Atlantic Canada.

💼 The Role
The ideal candidate will hail from a sales or account manager background with an interest in marketing and experience working within a collaborative team environment. The candidate will lead and execute Granville Biomedical’s sales and business development strategy, focusing on growing international sales and developing key partners

Higher Talent has partnered with Pilot° in their search for a Managing Director reporting to their St. John’s, NL office.

ABOUT THE ROLE: 

Reporting to the President & Executive Chair, the Managing Director (MD) is the senior operational and leadership executive responsible for the day-to-day performance, stability, and growth of Pilot°. The MD translates vision into execution, ensuring the agency operates profitably, efficiently, and with strategic clarity.

This role is ideal for a seasoned leader who combines business acumen, people leadership, and operational discipline, and who thrives in a creative, client-driven environment. The MD must be a confident, credible leader internally and with senior clients.

The Managing Director is central to Pilot’s next chapter—ensuring the agency is strong, stable, scalable, and future-ready, while preserving the values, reputation, and creative excellence that define the brand for more than three decades.

Pilot° is a full-service marketing and communications agency with more than 30 years of experience supporting long-standing clients across the private, public and non-profit sectors. Chief Administrative Officer

Higher Talent is excited to conduct an expedited search for a motivated and community‑focused

 Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) for the Town of Witless Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador.

WHO WE ARE: 

The Town of Witless Bay is not just a great place to visit but is also a great community to live and do business.  Located on Route 10 as a part of The Irish Loop, Witless Bay is a small, scenic, traditional Newfoundland outport community with a population of 1640 that offers an abundance of Natural Tourism. Hiking, camping, and fishing can be found along the East Coast Trail. Sea kayaking is very popular in the Witless Bay area. For the amateur looking for adventure, there are several businesses offering boat and sea kayaking tours in the area. Icebergs, whales, and incredible bird colonies are just some of the attractions this scenic area has to offer. The Witless Bay Ecological Reserve is a must see! To learn more about us, please click on the Town of Witless Bay.

THE ROLE: 

Reporting to the Town Council, we are seeking a motivated and community‑focused Chief Administrative Officer (CAO) which is a senior management position responsible for the strategic leadership and efficient delivery of all the Town’s administrative and operational services. Property Paralegal
Property Paralegal | Charlottetown, PE



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking an experienced Property Paralegal to join our Charlottetown office. The ideal candidate will bring a strong background in residential real estate transactions, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage files in a fast-paced environment. While we value collaboration and ongoing development, this role is best suited for someone who can confidently support property transactions with minimal supervision.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

Assist lawyers with all aspects of residential property files;
Draft documentation for property transactions;
Act and a liaison between the firm and clients, providing clear and timely communication on the status of transactions;
Manage ordering title searches and liaising with external partners (banks, other law firms, Realtors, IRAC and others);
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum two years of experienc

Senior Corporate Paralegal | Charlottetown, PE



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

This is a senior role suited for a confident professional who can manage corporate files with minimal supervision and provide guidance and support to lawyers and junior team members.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES


Working as part of a collaborative team, you will:

provide comprehensive support to the firm’s clients in all aspects of corporate transactions, including asset and share purchases and sales, reorganizations, and financings;
independently complete incorporations, amendments, continuances, amalgamations, dissolutions, revivals, and reorganizations of Provincial and Federal entities;
conduct and coordinate due diligence reviews;
prepare and file PPSA registrations and related searches;
review, organize, and maintain complex corporate minute books;
assist lawyers with transactional closings and post-closing matters;
record billable and non-billable time in accordance with firm standards;

title: AI Software Engineer
description:
Be a part of our success story. Launch offers talented and motivated people the opportunity to do the best work of their lives in a dynamic and growing company. Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. Your new trajectory starts here at Launch!
Launch is actively seeking qualified, energetic engineers with passion for building solutions leveraging new and emerging technologies related to AI. This is a software engineering role specializing in applications with use cases powered by AI solutions, especially Generative AI, such as LLM integration, vector embeddings, real-time inference, and semi-automated, human-in-the-loop workflows. This role offers an exciting opportunity to be at the forefront of AI technology, working on diverse projects that drive real-world impact. If you’re passionate about AI and have the technical expertise to back it up, this role may be perfect for you!

Write high-quality, maintainable code in languages such as Python, JavaScript, C#, or others relevant to AI development
Work closely with and in cross-functional teams including software engineers, project managers, designers, QA, data engineers, and data scientists
Integrate with a variety of different APIs, services, and technologies to bring pre-trained models and other technologies to bear, such as cloud-based vector databa

title: Delivery Lead
description:
Be a part of our success story. Launch offers talented and motivated people the opportunity to do the best work of their lives in a dynamic and growing company. Through competitive salaries, outstanding benefits, internal advancement opportunities, and recognized community involvement, you will have the chance to create a career you can be proud of. Your new trajectory starts here at Launch! 

The Role: 
As a Delivery Leader in our Management Consulting Studio, you will play a pivotal role in leading strategic engagements and driving impactful outcomes for our clients. You will serve as a strategic advisor—partnering with executive stakeholders to shape and execute transformational initiatives across industries. This individual will lead multidisciplinary teams, steward client relationships, and bring structure and clarity to complex business challenges. You are a trusted advisor with the ability to understand client needs, guide delivery teams, and translate strategy into actionable roadmaps. This is a strategic role that requires both business acumen and delivery excellence. 

Responsibilities Include:  

Serve as a strategic advisor and delivery owner for key client initiatives—shaping vision, approach, and execution plans

Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de demain.

About Us

Propel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The Role

Are you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?

We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.

This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems.

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
Be a part of our success story. At Launch, we offer talented and driven professionals the opportunity to deliver high-impact work in a dynamic, fast-growing environment. We invest in our people through competitive compensation, exceptional benefits, internal growth pathways, and meaningful community involvement. For senior professionals ready to shape the future of AI and Data Science, your next big opportunity starts here.

The Role:
As a Senior Data Scientist, you will lead the design and development of advanced machine learning and AI solutions that solve complex business challenges. You’ll apply a deep technical foundation in data science, spanning statistics, modeling, software engineering, and domain expertise, while helping set technical direction, mentor team members, and influence decision-making across the organization. You will work closely with stakeholders and cross-functional teams to define use cases, architect scalable solutions, and drive innovation through GenAI, LLMs, NLP, computer vision, and other cutting-edge techniques.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead advanced Data Science initiatives from ideation to deployment, ensuring alignment with business strategy and measurable outcomes.
Develop, implement, and optimize AI/ML models across a range of domains, including predictive analytics, GenAI, LLMs, time series, deep learning, and NLP.
Conduct rigorous exploratory data analysis and feature engineering to uncover insights and support robust model development.

Construction Management Representatives
On behalf of our client, SEM requires the services of up to four individuals to join its engineering and project management team to act as Construction Management Representatives (CMRs) to support the execution of an upcoming project regarding the remediation of access bridges along the Labrador Island Link (LIL) Transmission Line.

The work will require significant travel to remote areas and is expected to occur throughout the entire LIL Asset located between Soldier’s Pond and Muskrat Falls and the successful candidates may be required to work either in Labrador or Newfoundland. 

Additional support may be required for other projects in and/or around Churchill Falls, Muskrat Falls, Soldiers Pond and Holyrood. 
 
Key Responsibilities

Project Oversight & Coordination

Act as the primary on-site liaison between the Project Manager and contractors
Contribute to daily coordination meetings with operations staff and contractors
Coordinate work activities, outages, and site access with operations and other trades
Communicate scope changes, risks, and issues to the Project Manager

Monitoring & Reporting

Monitor contractor activities to ensure compliance with schedule, scope, and technical specifications
Maintain daily progress reports, including updates, issues

description:

RESOURCES

SENIOR PROGRAMMER

Do you dream of having a say in the software you write? Strong Data Automation is an old company with a start up mentality that currently specializes in waste management logistics. That’s garbage you say? Exactly! We are a tight team working to build essential, usable products for our customers quickly and everyone pitches in to get the job done right. You’ll work on problems of secure data collection, operation scaling and automation, and hardware integrations across multiple applications and geographical locations. Customers will love you for making their job about high level decision making instead of copy and pasting between spreadsheets. We handle tracking, billing, trending and more as we try to revolutionize how organizations control what happens to everything the world doesn’t want.

5+ years programming experience
 
Computer science degree or equivalent experience / education
 
Full stack programming experience working primarily in the .NET ecosystem
 
Familiar with modern development best practices and design patterns
 
Knowledge of key security fundamentals for web application development
 
Strong knowledge of SQL Server database
 
Thrives in an atmosphere of taking on new challenges and developing new skills

Careers
Careers at Virtual Marine
WHO ARE WE?

Virtual Marine is a privately held Canadian company with its headquarters in Paradise, Newfoundland and Labrador.  Our mission is to improve the safety of life at sea by providing realistic, effective, and safe training tools for small craft operators. Our simulators allow crews of small marine craft to train for real-life situations in environments that are often too dangerous and costly for live boat exercises. 

We’re growing our strong, cohesive, aligned team with a can-do attitude, that’s driven by our values. We foster an inclusive culture and it shows - staff turnover is lower than industry average. We celebrate wins, and take care of each other. 

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We offer a competitive salary and benefits package and a rewarding challenge with our dynamic and growing team solving big problems!

Interested in seeing some of our work? 
Visit our YouTube channel @VMTechnology or check out the rest of our website or social media.

Project Manager (new)

As the Project Manager, you will play a lead role in planning, coordinating and overseeing the successful implementation of client-based projects and deliverables. title: Cloud IT Consultant
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Cloud IT Consultant
Cloud - Calgary, Alberta (Remote)

Cloud IT Consultant, Cloud AWS Services
Full Time / Permanent





Ready to take cloud deployments to the next level? Join our fast-growing team as an AWS Cloud IT Consultant, where you’ll design, deploy, and optimize AWS environments for SAP Business One and SAGE clients.




You’ll work hands-on with Linux and Windows servers, manage web and application services, and support client go-lives—even after hours. Your expertise will help shape scalable, secure cloud systems that drive real business results.




What You’ll Do:

Deploy AWS environments for SAP & SAGE clients.
Configure and support web and application services (IIS, Apache, Nginx) and file/print services.
Lead go-live migrations, including after-hour cutovers.
Document build processes, adhere to policies, develop and maintain procedural documentation, and support continuous improvement.
Collaborate with clients, consultants, and stakeholders to ensure successful builds and deployments.
Monitor system and database backups to ensure processes are running as expected.
Complete environmental restorations and server maintenance.
Respond to security events and resolve issues. 
Identify potential issues and drive proactive solutions.





Experience Required for the Role:

5+ years’ AWS or other cloud deployment.
Strong Linux skills (SUSE preferred) and solid Windows/AD/Networking knowledge (VPN, Firewalls, AWS Security groups).

description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Cloud Experience Delivery Manager
Cloud - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador (Hybrid)

Cloud Experience Manager 

Full Time/Permanent  

Required Location: St. John’s, NL  (Hybrid: Office and Work-from-Home St. John's, NL)





Make our cloud delivery feel seamless, confident, and controlled.  

  

As Cloud Experience Manager, you will own the end-to-end client experience for cloud service delivery — from quoting and implementation through go-live and support transition. This role is equal parts coordination, leadership, and customer advocacy – including both delivery leadership and operational ownership. 

 

Headquartered in St. John’s, NL, you’ll lead a delivery team and collaborate across sales, engineering, delivery, and operations to support clients across North America, the United Kingdom, and global locations. 

 

The Role  

You’ll sit at the center of cloud delivery execution, service operations, and customer experience — driving alignment across teams and acting as the primary owner of the delivery experience. Your focus is ensuring cloud solutions are delivered smoothly, meet commercial and operational expectations, and transition successfully into live production with minimal friction.  

 

Key Responsibilities  

Own the end‑to‑end cloud delivery and go‑live experience, ensuring smooth and predictable customer launches.  
Partner with sales to align delivery timelines, scope, and customer expectations.  
Central lead for custome

title: Senior Recruiter
description:
Privacy Policy
Job Openings
Senior Recruiter
Talent - St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador (Hybrid)

Build your Career. Shape the Future. Grow With Us.

 

At Vision33, we help organizations transform how they work through strategy, technology, and innovation. With teams across North America, the UK, and beyond, we partner with growing businesses to solve complex challenges and deliver real results.




We’re growing our Talent Team and we’re looking for a driven, relationship-first Senior Recruiter who knows how to attract exceptional talent in a competitive market—and enjoys the challenge of finding the right people before anyone else does.




“Here, recruiting isn’t transactional—it’s strategic.” – President, Tony Whalen

 

You’ll partner directly with leaders, influence hiring decisions, build talent pipelines across global markets, and play a visible role in shaping the future of our teams.

 

If you enjoy sourcing top talent, love building relationships, and know how to turn great connections into exceptional hires in a fast-paced environment—we want to hear from you.

 

What You’ll Do

Partner with Vision33 leaders to drive hiring strategy and talent decisions – not just execute it.
Source and headhunt talent globally - Canada, US, UK, and beyond.
Build and nurture proactive pipelines for hard-to-fill and high-impact roles.
Deliver ‘Best-in-Class’ candidate and manager experience.
Create compelling job ads, outreach campaigns, and employer branding content.

title: Community Connections Officer
description:
Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 
 
Job Title: Community Connections Officer 
Location: Gander, NL 
Position Type: 1 full-time position until March 31, 2027, with possibility of extension pending budget availability and satisfactory performance
Closing date: Until filled 
 
Reporting to the Regional Settlement Coordinator, the Community Connections Officer is primarily responsible for, but not limited to, planning, organizing and facilitating community-based programs, activities and volunteer-led projects to provide newcomers with the opportunity to engage with the local community.  
 
Roles and Responsibilities: 

Identify, maintain, and build community partnerships to support newcomers and their families as they settle and integrate into the local community 


Deliver the Canada Connects and Community Connections Program 


In collaboration with the Regional Settlement Coordinator, recruit, train, and manage volunteers involved in programming in their respective regions 


In collaboration with the Regional Settlement Coordinator, deliver orientation and professional development sessions for volunteers in their respective regions 


Identity funding opportunities and assist in preparing proposals to mark key events (e.g., Canada Day, Multiculturalism Day/Week, Black History Month, etc.)

Job Title: Associate Director of Community and School Engagement 
Location: St. John’s, NL 
Position Type: 1 full-time position 
Closing date: Until Filled 
 
As part of the senior leadership team, the Associate Director of Community and School Engagement provides strategic leadership to a diverse portfolio of programs supporting the integration and well-being of newcomer children, youth and families. The Associate Director leads and mentors a multidisciplinary team, builds strong partnerships across the organization and with external partners, and develops inclusive, trauma‑informed, and community‑responsive programming. The role also contributes to organizational growth through involvement in funding proposals and the development of new initiatives aligned with community priorities.

description:
Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 
 
Job Title: Clinical Counsellor 
Location: St. John’s, NL 
Position Type: 1 Temporary Part-time 
Closing date:Until Filled 
 
Reporting to the Youth Mental Health Program Manager, the Clinical Counsellor provides clinical oversight, guidance, and therapeutic support within the Youth Mental Health Peer Supporter Program. This role ensures that peer support services are delivered ethically, safely, and effectively, while promoting youth empowerment, resilience, and independence. The Clinical Counsellor supports both program participants (youth) and peer supporters through supervision, consultation, and direct intervention when required. 
 
Roles and Responsibilities: 
 

Provide short-term, solution-focused counselling to youth participants as appropriate 


Conduct risk assessments, safety planning, and crisis intervention when needed 


Support referrals to external mental health and community services 


Ensure services align with trauma-informed, youth-centered, and strengths-based practices 


Provide clinical supervision and guidance to youth peer supporters 


Assist in developing boundaries, ethical decision-making, and role clarity for peer workers 


Support peer supporters in managing complex or high-risk situations 


Facilitate debriefing sessions following critical incidents

title: Senior Performance Marketing Manager
description:
Senior Performance Marketing Manager
US, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role owns paid distribution at CoLab. You’ll own the performance, growth, and evolution of our paid media program — with LinkedIn as the primary channel, but not the only one. Your job is to take what’s already working, improve it month over month, and expand into new channels that help us reach the right buyers in new ways.

That means running and scaling LinkedIn paid social, owning our paid newsletter program, and designing thoughtful experiments in emerging or underutilized channels (e.g., Reddit, YouTube, or other

Litigation Paralegal
Litigation Paralegal | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Litigation Paralegal to join our Halifax office to provide paralegal services to the Litigation Practice Groups.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Draft pleadings,
Utilize Litigation Support Software to manage document production, disclosure and organization, including the following tasks:
data entry to objectively code documents
preparation of Affidavits Disclosing Documents (including review and redact for privilege and relevance);
compile Books of Evidence/Exhibits;
document production;
import documents received from opposing counsel;
prepare legal chronologies;
Record all billable and non-billable functions on a daily basis;
Utilize computer technology in an efficient and productive manner;
General office and clerical duties, including billable and non-billable functions;
Other duties as required.
SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS

Minimum 5 years’ experience as a litigation paralegal/

The University of Lethbridge invites applications for the position of Associate Vice-President (AVP), Human Resources. This is a significant leadership opportunity to advance people, culture, and organizational effectiveness at a pivotal time for the University.

The Organization

Founded in 1967, the University of Lethbridge is a comprehensive academic and research institution recognized for its commitment to liberal education, experiential learning, and community engagement. With campuses in Lethbridge and Calgary, the University serves approximately 8,000 students and employs a complex workforce of faculty and staff represented across eight different employee groups. Guided by a new strategic plan and energized by recent academic program expansions, including the launch of a new medical program and the planned development of an engineering program, the University is poised for transformative growth.

Single Parent’s Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL)
St. John’s, NL

Step into a role where strong leadership meets real-world impact, guiding an organization that changes lives every day.

The Company

SPANL was founded in September of 1987 by Margaret Acreman. Based on her own experience as a single parent, she recognized the gaps in available services and little awareness of the issues facing single parent families.  Today, the Single Parent Association of Newfoundland and Labrador (SPANL) is a dynamic, non-profit organization dedicated to empowering single parents on their journey to overcome challenges and achieve their goals. Through a comprehensive range of programs and services—including education, emergency assistance, and community supports—SPANL fosters self-sufficiency and resilience in families across the province.

The University of Windsor is seeking an experienced and values-driven leader to serve as its Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO). This current vacancy is a pivotal opportunity to shape the future of the University through strategic, innovative, and collaborative leadership in support of UWindsor’s academic mission and long-term success.

The Organization

The University of Windsor is situated on the traditional territory of the Three Fires Confederacy of First Nations: the Ojibwa, the Odawa, and the Potawatomi.

The University is a comprehensive research and teaching institution with more than 16,000 students. Located in the heart of Canada's Border City, the University of Windsor combines excellence in teaching, research, and professional practice with a commitment to build stronger communities, treat people with respect and dignity, and address pressing societal needs.

SEM is currently seeking a full-time Junior Environmental Scientist.

The Junior Environmental Scientist will support the SEM team in completing project-based work for a variety of clients. This position will be based at our office in St. John’s, NL.

 
RESPONSIBILITIES

•    Environmental data organization and analysis
•    Desktop review and interpretation of scientific literature and regulatory framework
•    Participation in planning and execution of baseline studies
•    Support data compilation and calculation exercises for climate change projects
•    Technical report writing in a wide range of environmental specialities including, but not limited to, atmospheric science, noise, contaminated sites monitoring, water quality, environmental protection; interest to learn and perform tasks outside of area of skills and studies considered an asset.
•    Collaborate with a team of subject matter experts to deliver high-quality environmental services to meet deadlines
 

REQUIREMENTS

•    Post-secondary education in Biology, Chemistry, Environmental Science, or related field of study; completion of a thesis-based program considered an asset
•    1-3 years of relevant experience; consulting experience considered an asset

SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP 

Full Time / Permanent 
Location:
Open Canada – East Coast  
Language Requirement: English (Preference for English with minimum conversational French)
 

 

Ready to combine your SAP Business One expertise with your passion for craft beverages? 




Brutos by Vision33 is growing, and we’re looking for an experienced SAP Business One (Production-focused) Implementation Consultant to help breweries and distilleries streamline operations with our industry-focused ERP solution powered by SAP Business One. 




Brutos by Vision33 is a beverage ERP software that helps brewery and distillery manufacturers increase operational efficiency and gain 360-degree visibility into their business. It is an all-in-one ERP software that harnesses the power of SAP Business One to centralize data by combining front- and back-office functions.  




If you thrive in customer-facing ERP consulting roles and love solving complex production and manufacturing challenges, this could be your next great opportunity. 





Your blend of responsibilities: 

Lead full-cycle SAP Business One implementations, upgrades, and migrations for brewery and distillery customers.  
Guide clients through business process improvements and identify system gaps and optimization opportunities.  
Build strong customer relationships through ongoing consultati

Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

Proofread, edit, and format documents of the package, standardizing both content and layout with boilerplate templates in accordance with the client’s applicable style manuals, guidelines, and standards

Collaborate with the ILS Manager and the engineering team to resolve content

About Us

Propel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.

The Role

We launched the Propel Marketplace to connect our ~5M monthly active users to products, services, and exclusive offers from leading brands across grocery and retail, healthcare, telecoms, financial services, and auto. It has grown into a powerful, industry-leading performance tool for our partners and a critical revenue driver for Propel. We have strong product market fit, our users love our app, and our customers love that we have a significant reach and engagement with our users.

We’re looking for a Revenue Operations Lead! We're scaling revenue operations for a profitable marketplace - and we need someone who moves fast, thinks strategically, and is eager to provide proactive support to senior leaders.

You'll own the RevOps program end-to-end: embedding AI into our tech stack, surfacing insights that drive decisions, and serving as a trusted partner to the Customer Success and Business Development teams. You stay ahead of industry trends and hold yourself to a high bar.

Your Opportunity We are seeking a highly skilled and business-oriented Data Scientist to join the Tax R&D Lab within the Tax Transformation Office and help shape the future of how Tax operates across the practice. The Tax R&D Lab serves as the experimentation, innovation, and solution development engine supporting the broader Tax Transformation roadmap and Total Tax Ecosystem vision. Working alongside other functions within the Tax Transformation Office — including Transformation Relationship Management (TRM), Data & Analytics, and Innovation & Change (I&C) — this role will help identify, prototype, build, and scale intelligent solutions that modernize workflows, improve operational efficiency, and enhance client outcomes across Tax. PERMANENT · ADMINISTRATIVE & OFFICE SUPPORT
Pension Assistant

NSHEPP is one of the largest registered pension plans in Nova Scotia, serving over 40,000 active members and 17,000 retirees, with over 90 participating employers at 150+ sites throughout Nova Scotia.

On behalf of NSHEPP, we are recruiting a Pension Assistant to provide administrative support for Pension Operations. Reporting to the Manager, Pension Administration, the Pension Assistant will provide superior customer service to Plan members by providing accurate and timely information regarding their pension benefits, answering general inquires (telephone or e-mail) and ensuring all documentation for the members’ benefit entitlements are completed accurately. The Pension Assistant will provide office administrative support to the Pension Services team, will support all aspects of preparing documentation related to pension benefits entitlements and will support and assist staff in successfully achieving pension plan goals and initiatives.

title: Industry Strategy Lead
description:
Industry Strategy Lead
NL, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

Our Ideal Candidate

This role is a high-leverage operator reporting into the Director of Industry Strategy & Transformation, with direct exposure to revenue mentorship and account owners. This role exists to inject strategic execution into CoLab’s enterprise deals, sharpen focus to scale wins, translate platform capability into enterprise strategy, and ensure we maintain product market fit.

This is a deal-facing, customer-facing, and product-influencing role. In this role, you will drive consensus inside complex manufacturing organizat

Genesis Venture Manager, CDL Atlantic

At Genesis, we help build amazing companies. We back founders with the support, connections, and expertise they need to grow.

Genesis, in partnership with Creative Destruction Lab (CDL), is seeking a Venture Manager for a one-year maternity leave replacement position. Based at Genesis in St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, this role will support and report to the CDL-Atlantic team (operated out of Dalhousie University). Founded at the University of Toronto's Rotman School of Management, CDL provides startups with access to a powerful network of accomplished entrepreneurs and investors through a recurring nine-month, milestone-based mentoring program focused on developing scalable, science-based startups.

Headquartered in Bermuda, with long-standing offices in Halifax and Toronto, Allshores Limited is a publicly traded insurance, health, and wealth management organization and holding company listed on the Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX). Formed through the 2025 amalgamation of BF&M and Argus, Allshores brings together nearly 200 years of combined experience to create a stronger, unified organization serving communities across Bermuda, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America.

As they continue to evolve and grow, Allshores is seeking a Senior Corporate Secretary to play a key role in supporting the effective operation of the Boards of Directors across their group of companies. At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

KPMG Forensic helps uncover the facts and create clarity when people and organizations do not agree, or where financial and business behaviour does not comply with expectations. Our professionals can offer support after an incident to help our clients investigate allegations of fraud and misconduct, quantify losses in a commercial dispute, provide expert witness services, and recover damages. We also get involved before any incident has occurred - to help address exploitable weaknesses in corporate processes and systems. Our national team includes investigative accountants, business valuators, analysts, forensic technology experts, former police officers and others with specialized skills.

title: Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical
description:
Welcome to the Kraken Robotics Career Center

Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical

Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical

NEW CAREER OPPORTUNITY




Build your career with Kraken Robotics. Make a difference to others. If innovation drives you, and you want a career that makes a difference to the world, helping humanity overcome the challenges in our oceans – safely, efficiently, and sustainably – then come join us at Kraken Robotics.

 

Kraken Robotics is currently seeking a Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician, Electrical to join our team in Dartmouth, NS.




As a Sr. Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technician with Kraken Robotics, you will play a key role in the support, definition, and maintenance of Kraken's product portfolios. You will primarily be responsible to produce error-free technical drawings, 3D models, and design verification documentation. You will work within the established workflows of the drawing office, ensuring that all deliverables meet company and industry standards in a timely manner. In this role, you will also play a key role in our Engineering Change Process by executing changes based on approved scopes and that the metadata of inputs and drawings are properly maintained. Key roles and responsibilities include working directly with members of the Drawing Office Team.




Roles and Responsibilities

Mentor a team of Jr. Electrical Computer-Aided Drafting (CAD) Technicians and provide technical “how to”

Senior Policy Advisor
Policy, Program Development & Gov't Relations / Politiques, Élaboration des programmes, Relations gouv. - Toronto, Ontario (Remote)

Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses?




At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 

 

Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 

 

At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply.

**Ce poste peut être occupé entièrement à distance ou de façon hybride, si vous habitez près de l’un des bureaux de Mitacs à Montréal, Toronto, ou Vancouver. Veuillez vous assurer de présenter votre candidature pour seulement un poste près de votre lieu de résidence.**


Vous souhaitez faire partie d’une organisation propulsée par sa mission qui façonne le futur de l’innovation au Canada? Vous aimez évoluer dans des milieux collaboratifs où la créativité, l’amélioration continue et la pensée stratégique sont valorisées? Êtes‑vous une personne motivée par des responsabilités qui contribuent à accroître la compétitivité du Canada et à produire des résultats concrets pour les communautés et les entreprises? 




Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. Nous établissons des liens entre les chercheurs et chercheuses et les organisations dans toutes les régions du pays pour aider à résoudre des défis pressants et accélérer la croissance économique. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de dem

Marine Services maintains a stockroom where vital supplies are housed to ensure items are readily available for all vessels within the fleet.  The Labourer II is responsible for maintaining the Marine Services stockroom at the White Hills Depot ensuring it is in a clean and orderly manner.  The Labourer II  will assist with the standardized receipt, issuance and inventory control of materials and equipment which includes maintaining proper inventory records and certifications such as inputting all records of receipt of stock into the inventory system;  notifies suppliers and/or purchasing department of short shipments or over shipments; receives,  unpacks, checks and records receipt of goods;   maintains records incidental to periodic inventories; physically loads/off loads related Marine Services parts including picking up parts from local suppliers;  p

The incumbent inspects, cleans and performs minor routine electronic, plumbing, mechanical and hydraulic repairs and maintenance in accordance with departmental preventative maintenance schedules; assist the Chief Engineer to ensure that back-up systems are in operational order; may accompany the vessel as required during refit schedules and assists technical personnel in carrying out repairs and alterations; assists with tie-up lines and other routine work during arrivals and departures of the vessel; participates in security watches when the vessel is non-operational; assist with operation of portable wharf lighting operation and vessel shore based genset operation and maintenance; and performs other related duties as

company: AXIS Career Services-Association for New Canadians
title: Accountant
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Association for New Canadians is a non-profit, community-based organization dedicated to the provision of settlement and integration services for immigrants and refugees. 
 
Job Title: Accountant 
Location: St. John’s, NL 
Position Type: 1 full-time position 
Closing date: Until filled 
 
Reporting to the Senior Director of Finance, HR, and IT, the Accountant manages and optimizes financial systems, ensuring data accuracy, integrity, and integration. The role includes generating accurate financial reports, overseeing project and grant accounting, and supporting budgeting and forecasting. 
 
Roles and Responsibilities: 
 1. Financial Reporting and Data Management

Leverage Sage 300’s financial reporting tools to develop customized reports and dashboards that provide insights into program performance, budgets, and funding compliance 
Prepare accurate and timely financial reports, dashboards, and reconciliations 
Analyze financial data, monitor cash flow, and ensure alignment with budgets and funding requirements 


2. Project Accounting and Grants Management

Track income, expenses, and budgets for projects and grants to ensure compliance 
Reconcile financial transactions, review expenditures, and prepare audit-ready documentation 
Support program teams with cash flow analysis and standardized financial management processes 


3. Budgeting and Forecasting

Assist in preparing budgets, forecasts, and multi-year financial plans 
Monitor program budgets, perform variance analysis,

title: Insolvency Advisor, Personal Debt Solutions
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. Your Opportunity Our Barrie office is seeking an Insolvency Advisor to join the Personal Debt Solution team. This is a replacement role, and this position entails maintaining an in-office presence by completing the following duties: Perform initial consultations to assess the debtor’s situation and recommend appropriate options, training will be provided Assist debtors and creditors, both in person and by telephone, as it relates to bankruptcies and proposals Review interview forms, and prepare files/documents for client signing Community outreach, business development, presentations, and use of social media to promote personal debt solutions offerings. Careers
Records Management Assistant
Records Management Assistant | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Records Management Assistant to join our Halifax office and support the effective organization and maintenance of Firm records in accordance with established policies and procedures.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Organize and maintain physical and electronic records, including client files, correspondence, and legal documents.
Ensure compliance with document retention policies and procedures, adhering to requirements established by the Nova Scotia Barristers’ Society (NSBS).
Assist with the digitization of paper records, including scanning and indexing documents.
Coordinate the transfer and disposal of records in accordance with legal requirements and firm policies with external vendors.
Respond to requests for information and locate requested documents promptly.
Provide support to lawyers and staff regarding records management processes and procedures.

Regional Marketing & Communications Coordinator | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Regional Marketing & Communications Coordinator to provide day-to-day coordination and execution of the firm’s marketing and communications efforts across all provinces.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Assist with the development of creative assets by writing briefs, liaising with the firm’s creative agencies, and tracking timelines and expenses.
Write targeted, compelling and on-brand content for marketing and communications materials including website, social media, media releases, advertising and speaking notes.
Support the firm’s social media efforts, including drafting and posting timely content and engagement, graphics creation, monitoring engagement activity and maintaining a content calendar.
Make timely website updates, coordinate troubleshooting and regularly track website analytics to measure performance.

Regional Events Coordinator
Regional Events Coordinator | Halifax, NS



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time Regional Events Coordinator to support the firm’s event, sponsorship and practice group initiatives across all provinces.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Using best practices in event planning, provide end-to-end planning, execution and on-site coordination of regional client and internal events, such as seminars, webinars, receptions and conferences.
Create and maintain event management documents including timelines, budgets, checklists, attendance, dietary requirements and run sheets.
Oversee the contact management process with lawyers to develop and maintain up to date client mailing lists.
Write, design and distribute event invitations using the firm’s e-vite and related software, including tracking RSVPs.
Assist in the creation of post-event reports and provide key insights.
Evaluate practice group sponsorship opportunities and coordinate sponsor benefits inc

SENIOR SPECIALIST, DATA ANALYTICS AND INNOVATION

Senior Specialist, Data Analytics and Innovation
One (1) Vacancy
Permanent, Halifax, NS
Competition #48956

Our goal is to build a diverse workforce that reflects the citizens we serve. The Office of the Auditor General has an Employment Equity Policy and welcomes applications from Aboriginal Peoples, African Nova Scotians and Other Racially Visible Persons, Persons with Disabilities, and Women in occupations or positions where they are under-represented. If you belong to one of these groups, we encourage you to self-identify in your cover letter or resume.

Why Join Us?
At the Office of the Auditor General of Nova Scotia, we provide independent assurance to the House of Assembly on the government’s use of public funds and performance. With about 40 professionals, we’re small enough that you’ll quickly build meaningful relationships and have your voice heard, but large enough to give you exposure to complex, high-profile audits that make a real difference in areas like healthcare, education, social services, and public safety. For more information, please visit www.oag-ns.ca.

DONALD HILL FAMILY POSTDOCTORAL FELLOW – ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN HEALTH & MEDICINE

Dalhousie University invites applications for a Donald Hill Family Postdoctoral Fellowship in Artificial Intelligence in Health & Medicine. This is an exceptional opportunity for a talented and ambitious researcher to build an independent, high-impact research program at the intersection of machine learning and healthcare.

The fellowship is anchored in the Faculty of Computer Science, under the supervision of Dr. Frank Rudzicz, Dalhousie’s Killam Memorial Chair in AI and Canada CIFAR Chair in AI. The co-supervision team will be assembled around the candidate’s own research vision, drawing from partners across Engineering, Medicine, Health, and Science.

Research Scope
The fellowship is open-ended in research direction, within the area of machine learning applied to health and medicine. We are interested in candidates with expertise and ambition in one or more of the following areas — though we welcome compelling proposals that go beyond them:

Foundation models and large language models in clinical settings (EHR, clinical notes, patient communication)
Speech and language biomarkers for neurological and cognitive disease (dementia, Parkinson’s, depression, psychosis)

In this role, you will design and implement modern data engineering platforms using Microsoft Fabric, supporting organizations transitioning from traditional Microsoft data platforms to scalable cloud-based analytics architectures.

The successful candidate will have strong hands-on data engineering experience, consulting delivery experience, and the ability to work directly with client stakeholders to deliver production-grade data platforms.

This role is ideal for a senior data engineer with experience modernizing legacy Microsoft data environments such as SQL Server and SSIS into modern Lakehouse architectures.

Your future duties and responsibilities:

Data Engineering & Platform Development
Design and implement enterprise data platforms using Microsoft Fabric
Develop Lakehouse architectures using One Lake, Delta tables, and Spark
Build scalable data ingestion and transformation pipelines using Fabric Data Factory
Implement data transformations using PySpark and SQL
Design medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) for enterprise analytics environments
Optimize pipelines for performance, scalability, and cost

title: Director of Engineering - AI
description:
Director of Engineering - AI
North America, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role involves building and scaling one of the most strategically important product development areas in CoLab: our AI/ML teams.

You’ll lead three teams: Applied ML, AutoReview, and Operator - bringing them into a cohesive, high-performing product area. This group is operating with startup-level speed and ambiguity - in this role you will be responsible for keeping the pace while adding clarity, structure, and consistent execution. Success here looks like consistently shipping meaningful, reliable AI capabilities,

 HRBP – Middle East

Who are we?
At Global Maritime, our remit is to meet the challenges of the sea. What truly sets Global Maritime apart in the world of maritime engineering consultancy is the combination of the intellectually stimulating work itself, our deeply collaborative culture, and the uniquely supportive leadership. Global Maritime stands out as the premier destination for maritime engineers due to our unique blend and unparalleled technical opportunities – the opportunity to shape the industry's future on globally significant, cutting-edge projects – within a supportive and collaborative international community. Combined with expert leadership that actively mentors your growth within a trusting environment.
 
Welcoming you to our team
You will join a high-performing regional team covering Azerbaijan, the U.A.E., Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Egypt. Help Shape the Future of Hospice and Palliative Care on Nova Scotia’s South Shore

The South Shore Hospice Palliative Care Society (SSHPCS) is a community‑founded, donor‑supported charitable organization advancing compassionate, accessible end‑of‑life care for residents of Lunenburg and Queens Counties. Through the generosity of donors and in partnership with the Nova Scotia Health Palliative Care Services team, they support and promote compassionate care for individuals and their loved ones who are living with a life-limiting illness.

Through sustained grassroots leadership and community support, SSHPCS has played a pivotal role in improving access to palliative and hospice care, most notably securing five designated hospice beds at Fishermen’s Memorial Hospital. 

SSHPCS is now entering a pivotal new chapter and is focused on expanding palliative and hospice bed capacity across the South Shore and advancing toward a future freestanding hospice facility, a transformational opportunity that will reshape end‑of‑life care in the regio

At KPMG in Canada, our people bring their unique perspectives to Canada’s most important challenges. Here, you can build momentum that reaches beyond our business, develop skills for the future, and take ownership of your career with support at every stage. Join a firm where your career can make a difference.

 

Our Governance, Risk and Compliance Services (GRCS) professionals provide a range of advisory services to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the risk function, regulatory programs, regulatory compliance functions, operational risk, enterprise risk management programs and third-party relationships governance initiatives. We analyze and assess an organization’s business strategies and related business processes and systems to help management understand and address business objectives, regulatory requirements, risks, controls, and improvement opportunities. Our professionals bring both deep technical and industry experience, allowing clients to strengthen their key governance, risk management and compliance efforts while optimizing business performance.

The Risk and Compliance Transformation team is dedicated to helping clients operationalize and refine their risk management and compliance functions. This team has a global mandate and spans multiple industries, with a particular emphasis on Financial Institutions, where we focus on the review, design, enhancement, and integration of: Enterprise Risk Management; Operational Risk Management; Regulatory Compliance; Third-party Risk Management; Climate Risk Management; Enterprise / Operational Resilience; 1b Functions; and Control Testing Functions.

title: Principal CAD Developer
description:
Principal CAD Developer
North America, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

CoLab is seeking a Principal Developer to join our growing team. 

In this high-impact, hybrid role, you will work closely with our leadership team as a senior Individual Contributor (IC) and product thinker. Your primary focus will be on supporting strategic initiatives from a technical perspective.

You will help define our product roadmap by building working prototypes, pressure-testing initial assumptions, proposing potential approaches, and transforming technical unknowns into clear, actionable product direction.

Our Ide

title: Engineering Manager
description:
Engineering Manager
Canada, Remote
Apply
About CoLab

At CoLab, we want to help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is a cloud based platform for engineering design review. We make it easy for subject matter experts (SMEs) across your business to access, evaluate, and comment on 2D drawings and 3D models. Our built-in AI peer checker, AutoReview, scans designs for common errors or non-compliance with your standards and guidelines. AutoReview creates markups and comments on your files, in context – just like a human checker.

With CoLab, human SMEs and AI work together to help you make better decisions and improve designs faster. We automatically capture knowledge from across your global business that would otherwise be buried in emails, spreadsheets, slide decks, and unknown locations in Sharepoint or PLM. Then, we make sure every lesson learned and every design guideline is applied exactly when it matters.

Companies like Johnson Controls, Komatsu, Schaeffler, and Polaris have launched products 40% faster, cut BOM costs by 50%, and reduced quality escapes by 15% in 1 year.

About the Role

We’re hiring an Engineering Manager to lead Build Operator, one of the most strategically important product areas at CoLab.

Operator is our conversational AI agent for engineering teams. It helps users search engineering knowledge, understand complex design data, execute actions inside CoLab, and automate review workflows. 

A lot of th

Provincial Trainer
Provincial Trainer | New Brunswick



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm with the knowledge and experience clients rely upon for solid legal solutions. The firm prides itself on a collegial work atmosphere and supportive team environment.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

We are seeking a permanent, full-time, experienced candidate to join our team as a Provincial Trainer. This role supports the training needs of all Firm members in various practice groups.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Supporting program training needs of all new firm members during the onboarding process and beyond;
Setting up and hosting regular refresher training sessions, virtually and in-person, with firm members;
Scheduling and delivering one-on-one training sessions as required, virtually and in-person;
Working closely with the management team to assess training needs, develop training programs and material, and suggest improvements by identifying any gaps in the current process;
Ensuring our program training aligns with the firm’s strategic goals;
Communicating with IT on backend support issues as needed;
Report any areas of concern to the management team, and working collaboratively to find a resolution;
B

Corporate Services Assistant
Corporate Services Assistant | Charlottetown, PE



Cox & Palmer is a full-service, top-ranked Atlantic Canadian law firm known for providing exceptional legal solutions. We pride ourselves on a collegial and supportive work environment and are seeking a Corporate Services Assistant with prior corporate legal experience to support our corporate team in Charlottetown.

Applicants must have at least three years of experience in corporate legal support or corporate administration. Candidates without relevant experience will not be considered.

SUMMARY OF RESPONSIBILITIES

Attend to all of the steps required to complete extra-provincial registrations, annual returns and name registrations for firm clients and for out-of-province clients and partner firms.
Draft or assist in drafting a wide range of legal documents and correspondence, ensuring high quality, accuracy and comprehensiveness, such as audit letters, annual returns, amendments, incorporations and related change notices, extra-provincial registrations, dissolutions, electronic filings, PPSA searches as well as maintenance of minute books.
Act as a liaison between lawyers, clients and other firms, as well as internal departments and external affiliates including government departments as requested.
Establish and maintain a current calendar of events and a deadline reminder system for assigned files.

OverviewApplication
About Us

Propel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.

The safety net's strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.

Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don't.

Over 5.5 million Americans trust Propel today. We've grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We're building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.

About the Role

We're hiring a part-time Office & People Operations Intern to help keep our Brooklyn office running and our team well-supported. This role sits at the intersection of people ops, facilities, and administrative coordination - the kind of work that makes everything else easier.

Our current office manager is moving on to a new opportunity, and we're restructuring the responsibilities of the team and the
